member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds
member.summary: Various diagnostic IDs reported by this repo.
member.remarks: When adding a new diagnostic ID, add a corresponding suppression to the root Directory.Build.targets file, and add a documentation entry to docs/list-of-diagnostics.md.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds.Experiments
member.summary: Experiments supported by this repo.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw
member.summary: Defines static methods used to throw exceptions.
member.remarks: The main purpose is to reduce code size, improve performance, and standardize exception messages.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNull``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
member.param: Object to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is , or if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to be checked for .
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type.
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to which member belongs.
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrWhitespace(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws either an or an if the specified string is or whitespace respectively.
member.param: String to be checked for or whitespace.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the string is , or if it is empty.
member.param: String to be checked for or empty.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfBufferTooSmall(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the argument's buffer size is less than the required buffer size.
member.param: The actual buffer size.
The required buffer size.
The name of the parameter to be checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfOutOfRange``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the enum value is not valid.
member.param: The argument to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of the enumeration.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the collection is , or if it is empty.
member.param: The collection to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of objects in the collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentNullException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentNullException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String,System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
The value of the argument that caused this exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.
member.remarks: If the is not a , the current exception is raised in a catch block that handles the inner exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.InvalidOperationException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.InvalidOperationException(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less than min.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1
member.summary: Represents a span that's potentially created over a rented array.
member.typeparam: The type of objects held by the span.
member.remarks:
This type is used to implement a common pattern to improve performance when using temporary buffers. The pattern encourages the use of stack-based buffers when possible, which eliminate the overhead of garbage collection of buffers.
With this type, you make a speculative call to allocate a buffer. If the buffer is too large to fix on the stack, it is allocated from . If the buffer could be allocated on the stack, then no buffer is acquired from the array pool, and instead the caller is expected to use to get the buffer.

example.code: using var rental = new RentedSpan<char>(length); var span = rental.Rented ? rental.Span : stackalloc char[length];

member.name: F:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.MinimumRentalSpace
member.summary: The minimum size in bytes that triggers buffer rental.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The desired length of the span. if this value is %lt;= 0, no buffer is allocated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Dispose
member.summary: Returns a rented array back to the array pool.
member.remarks:
If no array was actually allocated from the array pool (when is ), then this method has no effect.
Calling this method multiple times on the same object is not supported, don't do it.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Span
member.summary: Gets a span over the rented buffer.
member.remarks: If no buffer was rented (because the buffer was deemed too small), then this returns an empty span. When a buffer isn't rented by this type, it's a cue to you to allocate buffer from the stack instead using stackalloc.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Rented
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether a buffer has been rented.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.NoopPooledObjectPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy that does nothing.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledCancellationTokenSourcePolicy
member.summary: An object pool policy for cancellation token sources.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledDictionaryPolicy`2
member.summary: An object pool policy for dictionaries.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledListPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for lists.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledListWithCapacityPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for lists with capacity.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledSetPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for sets.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory
member.summary: A factory of object pools.
member.remarks: This class makes it easy to create efficient object pools used to improve performance by reducing strain on the garbage collector.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreatePool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreatePool``1(Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.IPooledObjectPolicy{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool with a custom policy.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The custom policy that is responsible for creating new objects and preparing objects to be added to the pool.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateResettingPool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool for resettable objects.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
member.remarks: Objects are systematically reset before being added to the pool.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateStringBuilderPool(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a pool of instances.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
The maximum capacity of the string builders to keep in the pool. This defaults to 64K.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateListPool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of object held by the lists.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateListPoolWithCapacity``1(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances, each with provided .
member.typeparam: The type of object held by the lists.
member.param: The capacity of each created instance.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateDictionaryPool``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of the dictionary keys.
The type of the dictionary values.
member.param: Optional key comparer used by the dictionaries.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateHashSetPool``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of objects held in the sets.
member.param: Optional key comparer used by the sets.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateCancellationTokenSourcePool(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
member.remarks: On .NET 6 and above, cancellation token sources are reusable and this pool leverages this feature. When running on older frameworks, this pool is actually a no-op, every time a source is fetched from the pool, it is always a new instance. In that case, returning an object to the pool merely disposes it.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.SharedStringBuilderPool
member.summary: Gets the shared pool of instances.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification
member.summary: Represents a single classification which is part of a data taxonomy.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.None
member.summary: Gets the value to represent data with no defined classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Unknown
member.summary: Gets the value to represent data with an unknown classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.TaxonomyName
member.summary: Gets the name of the taxonomy that recognizes this classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Value
member.summary: Gets the value representing the classification within the taxonomy.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The name of the data taxonomy this classification belongs to.
The taxonomy-specific value representing the classification.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Checks if an object is equal to this instance of .
member.param: Object to check for equality.
member.returns: if object instances are equal otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.Equals(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Checks if an object is equal to this instance of .
member.param: Instance of to check for equality.
member.returns: if object instances are equal otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.GetHashCode
member.summary: Get the hash code for the current instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.op_Equality(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Check if two instances are equal.
member.param: Left argument of the comparison.
Right argument of the comparison.
member.returns: if object instances are equal, or otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.op_Inequality(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Check if two instances are not equal.
member.param: Left argument of the comparison.
Right argument of the comparison.
member.returns: if object instances are equal, or otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification.ToString
member.summary: Gets a string representation of this object.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute
member.summary: Base attribute for data classification.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute.Notes
member.summary: Gets or sets the notes.
member.remarks: Optional free-form text to provide context during a privacy audit.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute.Classification
member.summary: Gets the classification represented by this attribute.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationAttribute.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The data classification to apply.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet
member.summary: Represents a set of data classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The class to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The classes to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The classes to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.op_Implicit(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)~Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet
member.summary: Converts a single classification to a data classification set.
member.param: The classification to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.FromDataClassification(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification)
member.summary: Converts a single classification to a data classification set.
member.param: The classification to include in the set.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.Union(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet)
member.summary: Creates a new data classification set that combines the current classifications with another set.
member.param: The other set.
member.remarks: This method doesn't modify the two input sets, it creates a new set.
member.returns: The resulting set which combines the current instance's classifications and the other ones.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.GetHashCode
member.summary: Gets a hash code for the current object instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Compares an object with the current instance to see if they contain the same classifications.
member.param: The other data classification to compare to.
member.returns: if the two sets contain the same classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.Equals(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet)
member.summary: Compares an object with the current instance to see if they contain the same classifications.
member.param: The other data classification to compare to.
member.returns: if the two sets contain the same classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of this object.
member.returns: The string representation of this object.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet.ComputeHashCode
member.summary: Computes the hash code for the current object instance.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationTypeConverter
member.summary: Provides a way to convert a to and from a string.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationTypeConverter.TryParse(System.String,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse a string in the format "TaxonomyName:Value".
member.param: The input string to parse.
When this method returns, contains the parsed taxonomy name if the parsing succeeded, or an empty string if it failed.
When this method returns, contains the parsed taxonomy value if the parsing succeeded, or the original input string if it failed.
member.returns: if the string was successfully parsed; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.NoDataClassificationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates data which is specifically not classified.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.NoDataClassificationAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.UnknownDataClassificationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates data whose classification is unknown.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.UnknownDataClassificationAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder
member.summary: Adds redactors to the application.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder.Services
member.summary: Gets the service collection into which the redactor instances are registered.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder.SetRedactor``1(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet[])
member.summary: Sets the redactor to use for a set of data classifications.
member.typeparam: Redactor type.
member.param: The data classifications for which the redactor type should be used.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactionBuilder.SetFallbackRedactor``1
member.summary: Sets the redactor to use when processing classified data for which no specific redactor has been registered.
member.typeparam: Redactor type.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactorProvider
member.summary: Provides redactors for different data classifications.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.IRedactorProvider.GetRedactor(Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassificationSet)
member.summary: Gets the redactor configured to handle the specified data class.
member.param: Data classifications of the data to redact.
member.returns: A redactor suitable to redact data of the given class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactor
member.summary: Redactor that does nothing to its input and returns it as-is.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactor.Instance
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of this class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactorProvider
member.summary: A provider that only returns the redactor used for situations that don't require redaction.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.NullRedactorProvider.Instance
member.summary: Gets the singleton instance of this class.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor
member.summary: Enables the redaction of potentially sensitive data.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Span{System.Char})
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.
Buffer to store redacted value.
member.returns: Number of characters produced when redacting the given source input.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.String,System.Span{System.Char})
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.
Buffer to redact into.
member.returns: Number of characters written to the buffer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact(System.String)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.param: Value to redact.
member.remarks: Returns an empty string when is .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact``1(``0,System.String,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.typeparam: Type of value to redact.
member.param: Value to redact.
The optional format that selects the specific formatting operation performed. Refer to the documentation of the type being formatted to understand the values you can supply here.
Format provider used to produce a string representing the value.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.Redact``1(``0,System.Span{System.Char},System.String,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data.
member.typeparam: Type of value to redact.
member.param: Value to redact.
Buffer to redact into.
The optional format string that selects the specific formatting operation performed. Refer to the documentation of the type being formatted to understand the values you can supply here.
Format provider used to produce a string representing the value.
member.returns: Number of characters written to the buffer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.TryRedact``1(``0,System.Span{System.Char},System.Int32@,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Tries to redact potentially sensitive data.
member.typeparam: The type of value to redact.
member.param: The value to redact.
The buffer to redact into.
When this method returns, contains the number of redacted characters that were written to the destination buffer.
The format string that selects the specific formatting operation performed. Refer to the documentation of the type being formatted to understand the values you can supply here.
The format provider used to produce a string representing the value.
member.returns: if the destination buffer was large enough, otherwise .

member.summary: Gets the number of characters produced by redacting the input.
member.param: Value to be redacted.
member.returns: The number of characters produced by redacting the input.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor.GetRedactedLength(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the number of characters produced by redacting the input.
member.param: Value to be redacted.

member.name: T:System.Text.RedactionStringBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Redaction utility methods.

member.name: M:System.Text.RedactionStringBuilderExtensions.AppendRedacted(System.Text.StringBuilder,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Redaction.Redactor,System.String)
member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data and appends it to a instance.
member.param: Instance of to append the redacted value.
The redactor that will redact the input value.
Value to redact.
member.remarks: When the is nothing will be appended to the string builder.

member.summary: Redacts potentially sensitive data and appends it to a instance.
member.param: Instance of to append the redacted value.
The redactor that will redact the input value.
